title: Read the Places — waypoints
description: A place, at a position in the narrative, with the passage that puts it there, and an honest statement of how sure we are.

name.description: How the place is shown to a reader. Use the book's own name for it where there is one.

clock.description: 24-hour progress label. Books ordered by chapter will use progress_label instead — see docs/DESIGN.md §3.2.

character.description: Must be declared in book.json → characters.

coords.description: [longitude, latitude] — GeoJSON order, NOT lat/lon. Must fall inside the book's setting.bbox unless outside_setting is true.

outside_setting.description: Set true only for a deliberate excursion beyond the book's setting (e.g. Bourton, outside London). Never to silence a bad coordinate.

place_certainty.description: explicit: the author named it. inferred: you worked it out. inspired_by: a real place behind a fictional one. disputed: scholars disagree, and we show the disagreement.

certainty_note.description: REQUIRED unless place_certainty is 'explicit'. Say why you placed it there. Hiding a guess is what makes a pin map.

quote_anchor.description: A VERBATIM string from source.txt, appearing exactly once. The build finds it and derives the waypoint's position in the novel. If it is not in the book, the build fails.

passage.description: What the reader sees. Must contain the quote_anchor verbatim.

note.description: Two or three sentences: why this place matters TO THE NOVEL. Not what is there today.

simultaneous_with.description: Other waypoint ids the author deliberately holds in the same moment. Must be mutual, and must share a clock. Not merely 'about the same time'.

editorial_status.description: A book ships per-waypoint, not per-book. A draft can be visible-but-marked rather than blocking the release.

position.description: DERIVED — do not author this. The build computes it from quote_anchor's offset in source.txt.
